event_name: Shepherd Creative Arts Presents: Honk! Jr.
venue: Sewell Mill Library and Cultural Center, Marietta
blurb:
Sewell Mill Library and Cultural Center is hosting a one-night musical this Friday, and it is a good one. Honk! Jr. is the stage adaptation of The Ugly Duckling, and Shepherd Creative Arts is putting it on with a cast of young performers who have clearly put in the work.

This is a sweet spot for grandparents, parents of young kids, or anyone who wants to spend 90 minutes watching something genuinely cheerful. The story holds up because the message holds up: what makes you different is what makes you special. Hard to argue with that.

It is right here in East Cobb, tickets are likely very affordable, and it runs only 90 minutes. Check Eventbrite for details.
scoring_notes: Included for completeness as a genuinely local event at a community venue. East Cobb's audience skews toward teens and college-age kids rather than young children, which limits the family fit somewhat. Best appeal is to grandparents or community arts supporters. Lowest scoring of the eight but still a real, local event worth knowing about.

event_name: Summer Concert Series at Marietta Square
blurb:
The 40th annual Glover Park Concert Series kicks off Memorial Day weekend on Marietta Square, and this is one of those events that earns its place on the calendar every single year.

It is outdoors, it is free, and it is on one of the prettiest squares in metro Atlanta. Grab dinner nearby, walk over, find a spot on the lawn. The kind of Friday night that does not require a plan or a reservation, just showing up.

Forty years is not nothing. This series has outlasted trends, recessions, and a pandemic. There is a reason people keep coming back. Head to Eventbrite for the full lineup and mark your calendar now.
scoring_notes: Recurring series, which normally dings uniqueness, but the 40th anniversary gives it a milestone feel and it is a genuine community institution. Free, local, and very easy to attend on a holiday weekend. Good fit for the East Cobb family and empty nester mix alike.

event_name: Global Running Day with Big Peach East Cobb and ASICS
venue: Big Peach Running Co., East Cobb/Marietta
blurb:
Global Running Day lands on June 4 this year, and Big Peach East Cobb is celebrating a day early with a free community run in partnership with ASICS. It is right here on Johnson Ferry Road, which means no excuses about the commute.

This is not a race. It is a group run, which is a much lower-stakes way to get outside, meet people in your neighborhood, and do something good for yourself on a Wednesday evening. Big Peach events usually include demos, giveaways, and the kind of crowd that makes you feel like you should have started running years ago.

Free to join. Local as it gets. Check Eventbrite to register and grab your spot.
scoring_notes: Hyper-local event literally in East Cobb, which is a big plus. East Cobb skews active and health-conscious. Free and low-commitment, which lowers the barrier to entry. The Global Running Day tie-in gives it a reason to exist beyond a typical group run. Uniqueness is modest but local relevance is very high.

blurb:
Smith-Gilbert Gardens is celebrating the 10-year anniversary of A Garden with Wings, its signature seasonal butterfly and pollinator exhibit, with a full day of Pollinator Palooza events on Saturday, May 30.

This is a genuinely special one. Ten years is a real milestone for a locally loved exhibit, and the gardens are putting on a full program from 10am to 5pm. It is the kind of outing that works whether you go solo with a coffee, bring a grandkid, or make an afternoon of it with a friend who loves plants and quiet things.

Smith-Gilbert is tucked away and worth discovering if you have not been. Go while the butterfly garden is at its peak.
scoring_notes: The 10th anniversary angle gives this a milestone feel that lifts it above a typical recurring event. East Cobb's educated, homeowning audience tends to engage well with nature and arts programming. Appeals to the empty nester segment and grandparent crowd especially. Close enough to feel local.

event_name: Norman Connors and The Starship Band Memorial Day Event
blurb:
Memorial Day Monday does not have to end with traffic and sunburn. Norman Connors, the legendary drummer, producer, and bandleader behind some of the smoothest R&B and jazz of the 70s and 80s, is bringing The Starship Band to Perfect Note Atlanta for one night.

If you know, you know. If you do not, this is a good night to find out. Connors produced "You Are My Starship" and worked with Phyllis Hyman, Minnie Riperton, and Herbie Hancock. The man has a resume. This is the kind of show that people who love real music will talk about for a while.

Perfect Note is an intimate venue, which means this will sell. Do not wait on it.
scoring_notes: Norman Connors is a genuine legend with a long career, and this is a rare Atlanta-area appearance. The Memorial Day timing is smart for a newsletter publishing that week. Intimate venue raises the stakes. Skews slightly older in appeal but that is a good match for East Cobb's median age of 42 and empty nester segment.

blurb:
This one is literally in your backyard. Lassiter Concert Hall is hosting a full orchestral program that opens with the film scores from E.T. and How to Train Your Dragon before closing with Berlioz's Symphonie Fantastique, one of the most dramatic pieces ever written for orchestra.

The film score opener is a smart move. It gives anyone in the room a familiar entry point before the evening builds into something bigger. Bring a teenager who thinks classical music is boring. By the time the Berlioz kicks in, they will not be bored.

Tickets are typically very reasonable for events at Lassiter, which makes this an easy yes. Check Eventbrite for pricing and seats before they go.
scoring_notes: East Cobb skews educated and high-income, which correlates strongly with classical music and arts attendance. The film score component makes it accessible for families with teens. Held at Lassiter, which is deeply local to East Cobb. Strong all-around fit.

blurb:
Sister Hazel is one of those bands that 42-year-olds have strong feelings about, and that is not a bad thing. If "All for You" was on your playlist in the late 90s, you already know why this show is worth the drive to Sandy Springs.

The band has been putting out music for over 30 years and still tours hard. This is not a nostalgia cash-grab. They are a real live band that sounds good on a stage. City Green is an outdoor venue in the heart of Sandy Springs, which means good weather in late May and room to spread out.

Friday night, great weather, a band you actually know. That is a strong combination. Check the Visit Sandy Springs website for ticket details and get there early if you want a good spot.
scoring_notes: Sister Hazel hit their peak in the late 90s, which lines up almost perfectly with the median age of 42 in East Cobb. This is not a niche act for a niche crowd. It is a recognizable name that will land with both the empty nesters and the parents dragging their college kids home for the summer. Outdoor venue adds to the appeal.

blurb:
One night, one outdoor venue, 25-plus of Sandy Springs' best restaurants all in the same place. Food That Rocks is exactly what it sounds like, and it is a legitimately great excuse to eat and drink your way through a list of spots you have been meaning to try.

This is the kind of event that makes a great date night without requiring a reservation, a plan, or a debate about where to go. You just show up, wander, and eat. The hardest part is deciding what to try first.

Check the Visit Sandy Springs website for ticket details. These tend to sell, and for good reason.
scoring_notes: East Cobb at $118K median income is exactly the audience for a curated food and drink event. Strong date night appeal for the empty nester segment. Unique format (25+ restaurants in one place) gives it real can't-miss energy. Sandy Springs is an easy drive from East Cobb.
